input.user_input: Summarize the main protections under the Electronic Communications Privacy Act relevant to enterprise SaaS providers.

actual:
The Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 (ECPA) is the primary U.S. federal statute governing the privacy of electronic communications. For enterprise Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) providers, ECPA is highly relevant because it dictates how they must handle customer data, particularly when facing requests from law enforcement or third parties. 

ECPA is divided into three main titles, but the **Stored Communications Act (SCA)** (Title II) is the most critical for SaaS providers, as it governs data held in cloud storage.
